user.title: Meter Sensors
user.description: Select your home electricity meter sensors. The home power sensor is required. Demand sensors enable advanced demand-guard logic to prevent exceeding your monthly peak tariff threshold.

data_description.home_power_sensor: Real-time total power consumption of the home (W or kW).
data_description.current_average_demand_sensor: Provided by your energy integration. Enables advanced quarter-hour demand-guard logic.
data_description.maximum_demand_current_month_sensor: Highest demand peak recorded this month — used for monitoring only.

actuator.title: EV Charger — Control Mode
actuator.description: Choose how the EV charger receives its setpoint. Current mode (Amperes) is supported by most chargers. Select Power if your charger accepts a direct power setpoint (kW).

actuator_entity.title: EV Charger — Entity & Measurement
actuator_entity.description: Select the number entity that controls the EV charger. Optionally add a real-time EV power or current sensor. When a sensor is provided it is used for accurate home-power subtraction instead of estimating from the control setpoint. Power sensor is preferred when both are configured.

data.ev_power_sensor: EV actual power sensor (optional, W or kW)

data_description.current_actuator_entity: The number entity that receives the maximum charge current command.
data_description.power_actuator_entity: The number entity that receives the maximum charge power command.
data_description.ev_current_sensor: Real-time measured charge current. Used when no power sensor is configured.
data_description.ev_power_sensor: Real-time measured charge power. Takes priority over the current sensor.

limits.title: Charging Limits
limits.description: Set the maximum home demand threshold and EV charger limits. Phase count and line voltage are used to convert between power and current.

data_description.scan_interval: How often the integration recalculates and updates the EV setpoint.
data_description.max_home_demand_kw: The peak demand threshold you want to stay below (e.g. contracted grid capacity).
data_description.phase_count: Number of phases used by the EV charger (1 or 3).
data_description.line_voltage_v: Grid voltage per phase — typically 230 V in Europe.
data_description.min_charge_current_a: Charger will stop instead of going below this current.
data_description.max_charge_current_a: Hard upper limit for the charge current command.
data_description.current_step_a: Minimum adjustment granularity for the current setpoint.
data_description.max_charge_power_kw: Hard upper limit for the charge power command.
data_description.power_step_kw: Minimum adjustment granularity for the power setpoint.

error.missing_actuator_entity: An EV actuator entity is required.
error.missing_current_actuator: An EV current actuator entity is required when mode is Current (A).
error.missing_power_actuator: An EV power actuator entity is required when mode is Power (kW).
